turns.thought: [OBSERVE] At iteration 713, the swarm has completely converged: best fitness 19.899... unchanged for 200 iterations; diversity and position std are near zero (normalized diversity 2.8e-13, swarm radius 3.4e-11); all velocities effectively zero; all fitness values identical (std 3.5e-15). No improvement and extreme stall indicate the swarm is trapped in a local minimum of the Rastrigin function. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is premature convergence to a deep local optimum of the multimodal Rastrigin landscape. All particles collapsed to the same point, losing diversity, and the zero velocities prevent escape. This is not a boundary issue but a basin trap. The fitness ~19.9 suggests a local minimum, not the global optimum (0). | [STRATEGIZE] To escape the local basin, we need to redistribute particles widely while preserving the best solution as a reference. Levy flight with heavy-tailed jumps around the global best will place some particles far away to explore new basins while keeping others near the current best for refinement. A high ratio (0.5) and a large scale (0.5 of search span) will ensure sufficient diversity restoration. This is more targeted than random reinitialization and better at escaping multimodal traps than Gaussian noise.
